narrative:
Prediction market platform Kalshi has disclosed its first insider trading fines, targeting a MrBeast video editor and a California political candidate for violating platform policies [1][2][3].

## Key Facts

Artem Kaptur, an editor for YouTube creator MrBeast, was fined $20,397.58 and suspended for two years after Kalshi's disciplinary committee found he violated insider trading policies [1][2][3]. Kaptur traded approximately $4,000 on YouTube streaming markets in August and September 2025, generating $5,397.58 in profits [2][3]. Kalshi's surveillance systems flagged his "near-perfect trading success on markets with low odds, which were statistically anomalous" [3].

Separately, Kyle Langford, a California gubernatorial candidate, was fined over $2,000 and banned for five years after allegedly trading on his own candidacy and posting about it on social media [2][3].

## Company Response

Beast Industries, MrBeast's company, stated it "does not tolerate this behavior" and has initiated an independent investigation [2]. The company noted that contestants on MrBeast's Amazon Prime show "Beast Games" are also informed that confidential knowledge precludes them from participating in related prediction markets [2].

## Regulatory Context

Kalshi reported both cases to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission and noted it has opened more than 200 investigations over the past year, resulting in more than a dozen active cases [3]. The platform stated it will donate the fine amounts to a consumer education nonprofit [2]. These disclosures come amid growing regulatory scrutiny of prediction markets, with Nevada suing to block Kalshi and Massachusetts ordering the platform to stop allowing residents to place sports bets [3].

citations.title: Kalshi accuses MrBeast video editor of insider trading
citations.quote: The company's disciplinary committee found that Artem Kaptur violated its policies when Kaptur placed bets last August and September related to the YouTube channel where he worked.

citations.title: Kalshi fined a MrBeast editor for insider trading on markets related to the YouTube star
citations.quote: Kalshi says that Kaptur traded around $4,000 on YouTube streaming markets in August and September 2025. He made a $5,397.58 profit, prompting Kalshi to fine him for that amount, plus a $15,000 penalty.

citations.title: Kalshi says it fined a MrBeast editor and a politician for insider trading
citations.quote: Our surveillance systems flagged his near-perfect trading success on markets with low odds, which were statistically anomalous

keyDevelopments: Kalshi discloses first insider trading fines targeting MrBeast editor and California politician
Artem Kaptur fined $20,397.58 and suspended for two years for trading on MrBeast-related markets
Kyle Langford fined over $2,000 and banned for five years for trading on his own political candidacy
Beast Industries launches independent investigation into the matter
Kalshi reports cases to CFTC and reveals 200+ investigations conducted over past year
